# W003 should not fire when a {{with}} block calls a sub-template with the
# guarded (non-nil) value as dot. The sub-template's dot is known non-nil
# because it was passed from inside a {{with}} guard.

check-templates -w
! stderr 'W003'

-- go.mod --
module example.com/app

go 1.25.0
-- main.go --
package main

import (
	"embed"
	"fmt"
	"html/template"
	"net/http"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var source embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(source, "*"))

type Owner struct {
	Name  string
	Email string
}

type Page struct {
	Title string
	Owner *Owner
}

func handleOwner(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	_ = templates.ExecuteTemplate(w, "detail.gohtml", Page{
		Title: "Owner",
		Owner: &Owner{Name: "Alice", Email: "alice@example.com"},
	})
}

var _ = fmt.Sprint

-- detail.gohtml --
<h1>{{.Title}}</h1>
{{with .Owner}}
{{template "owner-card.gohtml" .}}
{{end}}

-- owner-card.gohtml --
{{define "owner-card.gohtml"}}
<div class="card">
    <p>{{.Name}}</p>
    <p>{{.Email}}</p>
</div>
{{end}}
